message:
feat: Add progress indicator component and improve TUI

Extend TUI functionality:

- Add progress component with progress_test
- Improve export/import handling
- Update model with new features
- Enhance spinner with improvements and tests
- Update Go dependencies
- Sync beads tracking state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -322,7 +322,7 @@
 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.35","title":"Task: UI toolkit guidance doc (gum/lipgloss/ratatui/web)","description":"Goal
- Document UI toolkit choices and version baselines for CLI/TUI/web subtools.

Details
- Write a concise doc that covers:
  - Shell UX with gum (optional, TTY-only, NO_COLOR fallback).
  - Go TUI with Bubble Tea + Lip Gloss (current versions and planned v2 upgrade).
  - Rust TUI guidance with ratatui (MSRV + edition constraints).
  - Web UI stack with Next.js 16 / React 19 / Tailwind 4 / Lucide / Framer Motion.
- Include version pinning guidance and update cadence.
- Provide migration notes for future upgrades.

Tests
- Lint/doc check ensures the doc is referenced from README or dev guide.

Success
- Contributors follow a consistent, high-quality UI toolkit approach across repos.
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:57:19.259683091Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T21:55:21.389382656Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.35","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:57:19.261206521Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.4","title":"Task: Status bar redesign + toast notifications","description":"Goal
- Make status feedback feel premium and always keep key hints visible.

Background
- Current status bar hides key hints whenever any status message is present, and all messages are styled the same.
- Skill guidance recommends a composited status bar with distinct chips and toast notifications.

Details
- Redesign status bar into segments: left (mode/context such as provider/profile/project), center (status message), right (key hints).
- Use keycap styles from the theme system for all hints.
- Add severity styling: success/warn/error colors and icons; keep hints visible even with messages.
- Add a toast system for transient success/errors (top-right or bottom-right), with timeout.
- Provide a config toggle for toasts and reduced motion.

Tests
- Unit tests for segment composition, severity styling, and hint persistence.
- Golden snapshots for status bar and toasts in light/dark themes.
- E2E TUI test that triggers success/error messages and verifies hints remain visible.
- Emit structured test logs with severity, message length, and toast timing.

Success
- Users always see navigation hints and can immediately distinguish error/success states.
","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.1 (theme system) and caam-fnkk (existing status color task).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:11:45.147497750Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T18:19:26.421922383Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T18:19:26.421856810Z","close_reason":"Implemented status bar redesign with 3 segments (mode indicator | center message | key hints) and toast notification system. Toast type with severity and auto-expiration. Mode indicator shows CLAUDE/SEARCH/HELP/CMD. Key hints always visible. 15 tests added for toasts and status bar.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.4","depends_on_id":"caam-fnkk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:11:54.254383192Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.4","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:11:45.148705905Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.4","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:11:49.924862011Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.4","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:34:50.781325107Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.5","title":"Task: Search bar + command palette UX","description":"Goal
- Make search/filtering visible and intuitive, and add a command palette for power users.

Background
- Current search mode has no visible input; users type blindly and rely on status text.
- Skill guidance highlights list filtering and command palette patterns.

Details
- Add a visible search bar component (textinput) that appears when search mode is active.
- Show live filter results, match counts, and a clear esc-to-cancel hint in the UI.
- Add a command palette (list component with filtering) presented as a modal overlay for actions like backup/activate/sync/import/export.
- Ensure search state is obvious in both compact and full layouts.
- Provide consistent keybindings for entering/leaving search and palette.

Success
- Users can discover and use search without guesswork; power users can execute actions quickly.","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.1 (theme system), caam-bxwa (search indicator task), and caam-l19o.1.3 (modal overlay for command palette).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:02.835728145Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T18:07:09.268656841Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T18:07:09.268570849Z","close_reason":"Implemented full Search bar + Command palette UX:

1. Search bar (from caam-bxwa): Visible search bar with '/' prompt, query display, cursor, match count, and hints (Enter/Esc).

2. Command Palette: New CommandPaletteDialog component added to dialog.go with:
   - Filterable list of 12 commands (activate, backup, delete, edit, login, open, project, usage, sync, export, import, help)
   - Up/Down/Ctrl+P/Ctrl+N navigation
   - Enter to select, Escape to cancel
   - Real-time search filtering by name, description, or shortcut
   - Scrollable list with selection indicator

3. Key binding: Ctrl+P opens command palette (added Palette to keyMap)

4. State handling: Added stateCommandPalette and handleCommandPaletteKeys

5. Tests: Added 8 tests for CommandPaletteDialog (Creation, Filter, Navigation, Select, Cancel, Reset, View, DefaultCommands)

All existing TUI tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.5","depends_on_id":"caam-bxwa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:11.330129088Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.5","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:02.836977408Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.5","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:07.979665683Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.5","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:20:57.751497795Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"caam-l19o.1.6","title":"Task: Motion for loading/progress (spinners + progress bars)","description":"Goal
- Add tasteful motion for loading and long-running operations to improve perceived performance.

Background
- Current loading states are static text (Loading…, Syncing…).
- Skill guidance recommends spinners/progress for async operations.

Details
- Introduce bubbles/spinner for sync, usage, import/export preview, and watch events.
- Add progress bars for long operations (bundle export/import, sync steps) when counts are known.
- Ensure motion is disabled when NO_COLOR or reduced-motion config is set.
- Centralize spinner and progress styles in the theme layer for consistency.

Success
- Users always see activity feedback; UI feels alive without being noisy.","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.1 (theme system) and caam-93pq (existing spinner task).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:20.569421371Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:23:20.009911577Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.6","depends_on_id":"caam-93pq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:29.594022908Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.6","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:20.570525751Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.6","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:26.171206211Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.6","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:34:53.595060378Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"caam-l19o.1.6","title":"Task: Motion for loading/progress (spinners + progress bars)","description":"Goal
- Add tasteful motion for loading and long-running operations to improve perceived performance.

Background
- Current loading states are static text (Loading…, Syncing…).
- Skill guidance recommends spinners/progress for async operations.

Details
- Introduce bubbles/spinner for sync, usage, import/export preview, and watch events.
- Add progress bars for long operations (bundle export/import, sync steps) when counts are known.
- Ensure motion is disabled when NO_COLOR or reduced-motion config is set.
- Centralize spinner and progress styles in the theme layer for consistency.

Success
- Users always see activity feedback; UI feels alive without being noisy.","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.1 (theme system) and caam-93pq (existing spinner task).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:20.569421371Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:36:07.756230501Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.6","depends_on_id":"caam-93pq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:29.594022908Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.6","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:20.570525751Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.6","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:26.171206211Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.6","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:34:53.595060378Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.7","title":"Task: Help/onboarding refresh with Glamour","description":"Goal
- Provide a polished, discoverable help experience with Markdown rendering and contextual shortcuts.

Background
- Current help view is functional but plain; skill guidance recommends Glamour for rich markdown help and structured key hints.

Details
- Use Glamour to render a concise help page (shortcuts + workflows).
- Cache rendered markdown to avoid re-rendering every frame.
- Add contextual help: show relevant hints based on current panel/state.
- Keep short help always visible in the status bar; full help via ? toggles.
- Ensure help is readable in light/dark themes and in NO_COLOR mode.

Success
- New users can learn the TUI in under a minute without leaving the app.","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.1 (theme system) and caam-s5bk (breadcrumbs/context UX).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:37.360331617Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:50:30.914161142Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T09:50:30.914111859Z","close_reason":"Implemented Glamour markdown rendering for help view. Added HelpRenderer with caching, contextual help hints for different states, and RenderHintBar for status line. Full test coverage with light/dark/NO_COLOR mode support.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.7","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:37.361991573Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.7","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:40.878804784Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.7","depends_on_id":"caam-s5bk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:46.754970484Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.8","title":"Task: Focus management + active panel highlighting","description":"Goal
- Make it obvious which panel is active and improve keyboard navigation clarity.

Background
- The UI currently uses selection highlights but lacks a strong focus ring for active panel/region.
- Skill guidance recommends clear focus cues and keyboard hints.

Details
- Add a focus state for provider/profiles/detail panels (border accent, title highlight).
- Show active panel name in the status bar and contextual shortcuts using keycap styles.
- Ensure tab/shift+tab cycles focus in a predictable order.
- Keep focus visibility in both compact and full layouts.

Success
- Users always know where their input is going and how to switch focus.","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.1 (theme system) and caam-l19o.1.2 (layout).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:53.867797053Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:42:28.423296936Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.8","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:53.869090629Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.8","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:57.813704114Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.8","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:13:01.252470419Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.9","title":"Task: Mouse + viewport support for long content","description":"Goal
- Add mouse support and smooth scrolling for long lists and detail content.

Background
- Skill guidance recommends mouse support and viewport for long content to feel premium.

Details
- Enable tea.WithMouseCellMotion in TUI Run and handle mouse wheel for scrolling.
- Use viewport for long detail descriptions and help content.
- Highlight rows on hover where feasible without breaking keyboard navigation.
- Provide config to disable mouse support for minimal terminals.

Success
- Users can scroll and interact with the TUI using mouse in modern terminals.","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.2 (layout) and caam-l19o.1.1 (theme system).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:13:08.455691417Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:22:07.857086136Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.9","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:13:08.457377292Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.9","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:13:15.457495418Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.9","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:13:11.997270089Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,471 @@
+package tui
+
+import (
+	"os"
+	"testing"
+
+	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/progress"
+	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
+	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
+	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
+)
+
+func TestProgressOptionsFromEnv(t *testing.T) {
+	envKeys := []string{
+		"NO_COLOR",
+		"TERM",
+		"REDUCED_MOTION",
+		"CAAM_TUI_REDUCED_MOTION",
+		"CAAM_REDUCED_MOTION",
+		"REDUCE_MOTION",
+		"CAAM_REDUCE_MOTION",
+	}
+	orig := make(map[string]string)
+	origSet := make(map[string]bool)
+	for _, key := range envKeys {
+		if val, ok := os.LookupEnv(key); ok {
+			orig[key] = val
+			origSet[key] = true
+		}
+	}
+	defer func() {
+		for _, key := range envKeys {
+			if origSet[key] {
+				os.Setenv(key, orig[key])
+			} else {
+				os.Unsetenv(key)
+			}
+		}
+	}()
+
+	tests := []struct {
+		name               string
+		envVars            map[string]string
+		expectNoColor      bool
+		expectReduceMotion bool
+	}{
+		{
+			name:               "default (no env vars)",
+			envVars:            map[string]string{},
+			expectNoColor:      false,
+			expectReduceMotion: false,
+		},
+		{
+			name:               "NO_COLOR set",
+			envVars:            map[string]string{"NO_COLOR": "1"},
+			expectNoColor:      true,
+			expectReduceMotion: false,
+		},
+		{
+			name:               "TERM=dumb",
+			envVars:            map[string]string{"TERM": "dumb"},
+			expectNoColor:      true,
+			expectReduceMotion: false,
+		},
+		{
+			name:               "REDUCE_MOTION set",
+			envVars:            map[string]string{"REDUCE_MOTION": "1"},
+			expectNoColor:      false,
+			expectReduceMotion: true,
+		},
+		{
+			name:               "REDUCED_MOTION set",
+			envVars:            map[string]string{"REDUCED_MOTION": "1"},
+			expectNoColor:      false,
+			expectReduceMotion: true,
+		},
+		{
+			name:               "CAAM_TUI_REDUCED_MOTION set",
+			envVars:            map[string]string{"CAAM_TUI_REDUCED_MOTION": "true"},
+			expectNoColor:      false,
+			expectReduceMotion: true,
+		},
+		{
+			name:               "both NO_COLOR and REDUCE_MOTION",
+			envVars:            map[string]string{"NO_COLOR": "1", "REDUCE_MOTION": "1"},
+			expectNoColor:      true,
+			expectReduceMotion: true,
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			// Clear all relevant env vars
+			for _, key := range envKeys {
+				os.Unsetenv(key)
+			}
+
+			// Set test env vars
+			for k, v := range tt.envVars {
+				os.Setenv(k, v)
+			}
+
+			opts := ProgressOptionsFromEnv()
+			assert.Equal(t, tt.expectNoColor, opts.NoColor, "NoColor mismatch")
+			assert.Equal(t, tt.expectReduceMotion, opts.ReduceMotion, "ReduceMotion mismatch")
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestNewProgress(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name    string
+		opts    ProgressOptions
+		checkFn func(t *testing.T, p *Progress)
+	}{
+		{
+			name: "default progress bar",
+			opts: ProgressOptions{
+				Width: 40,
+			},
+			checkFn: func(t *testing.T, p *Progress) {
+				require.NotNil(t, p)
+				assert.Equal(t, 40, p.width)
+				assert.False(t, p.noColor)
+				assert.False(t, p.reduceMotion)
+				assert.True(t, p.IsAnimated())
+			},
+		},
+		{
+			name: "progress with NoColor",
+			opts: ProgressOptions{
+				Width:   30,
+				NoColor: true,
+			},
+			checkFn: func(t *testing.T, p *Progress) {
+				require.NotNil(t, p)
+				assert.True(t, p.noColor)
+				assert.True(t, p.IsAnimated())
+			},
+		},
+		{
+			name: "progress with ReduceMotion",
+			opts: ProgressOptions{
+				Width:        40,
+				ReduceMotion: true,
+			},
+			checkFn: func(t *testing.T, p *Progress) {
+				require.NotNil(t, p)
+				assert.True(t, p.reduceMotion)
+				assert.False(t, p.IsAnimated())
+			},
+		},
+		{
+			name: "progress with custom color",
+			opts: ProgressOptions{
+				Width: 50,
+				Color: lipgloss.Color("#ff0000"),
+			},
+			checkFn: func(t *testing.T, p *Progress) {
+				require.NotNil(t, p)
+				assert.Equal(t, 50, p.width)
+				assert.True(t, p.IsAnimated())
+			},
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			p := NewProgress(tt.opts)
+			tt.checkFn(t, p)
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestNewProgressWithTheme(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name       string
+		themeOpts  ThemeOptions
+		width      int
+		expectAnim bool
+	}{
+		{
+			name:       "default theme",
+			themeOpts:  DefaultThemeOptions(),
+			width:      40,
+			expectAnim: true,
+		},
+		{
+			name:       "NoColor theme",
+			themeOpts:  ThemeOptions{NoColor: true},
+			width:      30,
+			expectAnim: true,
+		},
+		{
+			name:       "Reduced motion theme",
+			themeOpts:  ThemeOptions{ReducedMotion: true},
+			width:      40,
+			expectAnim: false,
+		},
+		{
+			name:       "high contrast theme",
+			themeOpts:  ThemeOptions{Contrast: ContrastHigh},
+			width:      50,
+			expectAnim: true,
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			theme := NewTheme(tt.themeOpts)
+			p := NewProgressWithTheme(theme, tt.width)
+
+			require.NotNil(t, p)
+			assert.Equal(t, tt.width, p.width)
+			assert.Equal(t, tt.expectAnim, p.IsAnimated())
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestProgressSetPercent(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name         string
+		opts         ProgressOptions
+		percent      float64
+		expectCmd    bool
+		expectStored float64
+	}{
+		{
+			name:         "animated progress returns command",
+			opts:         ProgressOptions{Width: 40},
+			percent:      0.5,
+			expectCmd:    true,
+			expectStored: 0.5,
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "reduced motion returns nil",
+			opts:         ProgressOptions{Width: 40, ReduceMotion: true},
+			percent:      0.75,
+			expectCmd:    false,
+			expectStored: 0.75,
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "clamps to 0",
+			opts:         ProgressOptions{Width: 40},
+			percent:      -0.5,
+			expectCmd:    true,
+			expectStored: 0,
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "clamps to 1",
+			opts:         ProgressOptions{Width: 40},
+			percent:      1.5,
+			expectCmd:    true,
+			expectStored: 1,
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			p := NewProgress(tt.opts)
+			cmd := p.SetPercent(tt.percent)
+
+			if tt.expectCmd {
+				assert.NotNil(t, cmd, "expected SetPercent to return a command")
+			} else {
+				assert.Nil(t, cmd, "expected SetPercent to return nil")
+			}
+
+			assert.Equal(t, tt.expectStored, p.Percent())
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestProgressSetPercentInstant(t *testing.T) {
+	p := NewProgress(ProgressOptions{Width: 40})
+
+	p.SetPercentInstant(0.5)
+	assert.Equal(t, 0.5, p.Percent())
+
+	p.SetPercentInstant(0.8)
+	assert.Equal(t, 0.8, p.Percent())
+
+	// Test clamping
+	p.SetPercentInstant(-1)
+	assert.Equal(t, 0.0, p.Percent())
+
+	p.SetPercentInstant(2)
+	assert.Equal(t, 1.0, p.Percent())
+}
+
+func TestProgressView(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name    string
+		opts    ProgressOptions
+		percent float64
+	}{
+		{
+			name:    "renders at 0%",
+			opts:    ProgressOptions{Width: 20},
+			percent: 0,
+		},
+		{
+			name:    "renders at 50%",
+			opts:    ProgressOptions{Width: 20},
+			percent: 0.5,
+		},
+		{
+			name:    "renders at 100%",
+			opts:    ProgressOptions{Width: 20},
+			percent: 1.0,
+		},
+		{
+			name:    "NoColor progress renders",
+			opts:    ProgressOptions{Width: 20, NoColor: true},
+			percent: 0.5,
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			p := NewProgress(tt.opts)
+			p.SetPercentInstant(tt.percent)
+			view := p.View()
+
+			assert.NotEmpty(t, view, "progress view should not be empty")
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestProgressViewWithPercent(t *testing.T) {
+	p := NewProgress(ProgressOptions{Width: 20})
+	p.SetPercentInstant(0.5)
+
+	view := p.ViewWithPercent()
+	assert.NotEmpty(t, view)
+	// The view should contain the progress bar and percentage
+	assert.Contains(t, view, "%")
+}
+
+func TestProgressSetWidth(t *testing.T) {
+	p := NewProgress(ProgressOptions{Width: 40})
+	assert.Equal(t, 40, p.Width())
+
+	p.SetWidth(60)
+	assert.Equal(t, 60, p.Width())
+
+	// Setting width to 0 or negative should be ignored
+	p.SetWidth(0)
+	assert.Equal(t, 60, p.Width())
+
+	p.SetWidth(-10)
+	assert.Equal(t, 60, p.Width())
+}
+
+func TestProgressUpdate(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("processes frame messages", func(t *testing.T) {
+		p := NewProgress(ProgressOptions{Width: 40})
+		require.NotNil(t, p)
+
+		// Create a progress frame message
+		frameMsg := progress.FrameMsg{}
+
+		newP, _ := p.Update(frameMsg)
+		assert.NotNil(t, newP)
+	})
+
+	t.Run("ignores other messages", func(t *testing.T) {
+		p := NewProgress(ProgressOptions{Width: 40})
+		require.NotNil(t, p)
+
+		newP, cmd := p.Update("random message")
+		assert.NotNil(t, newP)
+		assert.Nil(t, cmd)
+	})
+}
+
+func TestProgressNilSafety(t *testing.T) {
+	var p *Progress
+
+	// All methods should be nil-safe
+	assert.NotPanics(t, func() { p.Init() })
+	assert.NotPanics(t, func() { p.Update(nil) })
+	assert.NotPanics(t, func() { p.View() })
+	assert.NotPanics(t, func() { p.ViewWithPercent() })
+	assert.NotPanics(t, func() { p.SetPercent(0.5) })
+	assert.NotPanics(t, func() { p.SetPercentInstant(0.5) })
+	assert.NotPanics(t, func() { p.Percent() })
+	assert.NotPanics(t, func() { p.SetWidth(40) })
+	assert.NotPanics(t, func() { p.Width() })
+	assert.NotPanics(t, func() { p.IsAnimated() })
+
+	assert.Equal(t, "", p.View())
+	assert.Equal(t, "", p.ViewWithPercent())
+	assert.Equal(t, 0.0, p.Percent())
+	assert.Equal(t, 0, p.Width())
+	assert.False(t, p.IsAnimated())
+	assert.Nil(t, p.Init())
+	assert.Nil(t, p.SetPercent(0.5))
+
+	newP, cmd := p.Update(nil)
+	assert.Nil(t, newP)
+	assert.Nil(t, cmd)
+}
+
+func TestProgressWithLabel(t *testing.T) {
+	theme := NewTheme(DefaultThemeOptions())
+
+	t.Run("creates labeled progress", func(t *testing.T) {
+		p := NewProgressWithLabel(theme, "Exporting:", 30)
+		require.NotNil(t, p)
+		assert.Equal(t, "Exporting:", p.Label)
+		assert.NotNil(t, p.Progress)
+	})
+
+	t.Run("sets percent", func(t *testing.T) {
+		p := NewProgressWithLabel(theme, "Loading:", 30)
+		cmd := p.SetPercent(0.5)
+		// Animated progress returns a command
+		assert.NotNil(t, cmd)
+		assert.Equal(t, 0.5, p.Progress.Percent())
+	})
+
+	t.Run("renders view with label", func(t *testing.T) {
+		p := NewProgressWithLabel(theme, "Processing:", 30)
+		p.Progress.SetPercentInstant(0.5)
+		view := p.View()
+		assert.Contains(t, view, "Processing:")
+	})
+
+	t.Run("handles nil", func(t *testing.T) {
+		var p *ProgressWithLabel
+		assert.NotPanics(t, func() { p.SetPercent(0.5) })
+		assert.NotPanics(t, func() { p.View() })
+		assert.NotPanics(t, func() { p.Update(nil) })
+		assert.Equal(t, "", p.View())
+	})
+}
+
+func TestProgressEnabled(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("enabled with default theme", func(t *testing.T) {
+		theme := NewTheme(DefaultThemeOptions())
+		assert.True(t, progressEnabled(theme))
+	})
+
+	t.Run("disabled with reduced motion", func(t *testing.T) {
+		theme := NewTheme(ThemeOptions{ReducedMotion: true})
+		assert.False(t, progressEnabled(theme))
+	})
+}
+
+func TestProgressStyle(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("returns style with color", func(t *testing.T) {
+		theme := NewTheme(DefaultThemeOptions())
+		style := progressStyle(theme)
+		assert.NotNil(t, style)
+	})
+
+	t.Run("returns empty style with NoColor", func(t *testing.T) {
+		theme := NewTheme(ThemeOptions{NoColor: true})
+		style := progressStyle(theme)
+		assert.NotNil(t, style)
+	})
+}
+
+func TestDefaultProgressOptions(t *testing.T) {
+	opts := DefaultProgressOptions()
+	assert.Equal(t, 40, opts.Width)
+	assert.True(t, opts.ShowPercentage)
+	assert.False(t, opts.NoColor)
+	assert.False(t, opts.ReduceMotion)
+}
